Niko, Murphy was dead for 5 seconds
still count

SHE WASN'T DEAD YET

goliath as well
nah goli cut off his head
wait his head came off? i thought in his backstory it just hurts like hell a bunch
not in his story, but miryams
she told him to cut off his own head
i'd have to check the new translation, but in the old one his head doesn't come off
Cheese and crackers thats a lot..
hmm in that case it might be a Murphy-like case as well
maybe he didn't die
i think it's similar to what the sculptors did to 24
artificially created awakener as opposed to revived
i wont say any method is more artificial than the other
that's what keeper calls it in the story
thats why i use the 3 category system for awakeners
to contrast it with mythag's method
they're unsure if they can link with 24
in the archive some of the mythag awakeners call themself "silver chip technology" as a self deprecating joke
and when they do they're like. oh shit the sculptors can make artificial awakeners???
i can find the exact line later
i mean they're both artificial i think the keeper just means 'without dying'
since we're on the topic do yall think uvhash counts as an Entity or Revived type awakener since we know nothing about him while (if) he was alive
revived I think since he was made from a bunch of corpses iirc
closest I can find to that lore
but by then the corpses would count as material
i mean in the sense of "was his silver from a human"?
daff requires human heads too but her silver is her own so she falls under Entity
given that we've heard nothing about uvhash's pre-awakener life I'm guessing that he's like a gestalt awakener
a consciousness formed from the silver of a bunch of corpses
i mean he could be something else but until we get something more concrete i don't think we can know
hmm Entity leaning for now then
I don’t think it count as revived
those corpses are just the catalyst for the summon ritual

and besides 
he’s one of the few awakeners that using the exact deity name
i mean there's not really an answer one way or another, it's vague enough that either could be true
Ryker shot himself in the head
N just kept him going because he thought it was funny

If I remember it right, Ryker is dead after that bullet but somehow managed to speak with N in soul form so yeah
Ryker luck is sth he naturally has (as if a deity blessed him) and not N shenanigans 
so even if N wasn’t at fhe scene of the crime Ryker would still become an awakener
Just caught up with the main story, any guesses on why ||GTulu is specifically waiting for the keeper though?||
||I had expected him to be more callous based off the arc trailer but he ended up talking pretty similarly to normal Tulu, was surprised that he even went out of his way to apologize to Doresain.||
||based on what we know about tulu, he is aware about the nature of the Lemurian gods and that they stopped responding a long time ago||
||perhaps Gtulu is a tulu that actively maintained the farce of lemuria instead of trying to let it be dead for good?||
||we know he lied to miryam for morale sake but its something he regrets doing, plus it backfired on him in ch5||

||That's about what I expect from what little we've seen of him though if he can still see dreams of the future and seems more powerful in this reality, I wonder what changed to make him commit. Still not sure why he'd need the keeper for that though, unless he thinks their powers would help his cause||
i think some of those questions might stand a legit chance of being answered in the next chapter
True!
I guess just because he's about a year away doesn't mean we won't get some more info early
I’ve heard that the realms are based on different types of horror, with Aequor being lovecraftian and Caro being gothic. But I’m wondering what Ultra and Chaos are based on?
Idk where you got thatm
Then Ultra should be Cosmic
And Chaos is anything since their the blend
The smoothie of realms
not sure where you heard that, I wouldn't say it's particularly accurate
the entire game is lovecraftian, aequor specifically is centered entirely around Cthulhu
caro is just stuff pertaining to body horror/flesh and blood in general, ultra is dimensional things, and chaos is non-denominational
you can say they are different types of horror if you count it as random bullshit/fear of the ocean/ fear of the flesh/ freaky dimension shit

honestly the horror they use in realm ingame sometimes can be traced to the same genre
like reproduction horror with both Murphy and Thais
I don’t think we should be too strict or too loose about what kind of horror the realms represent
I can see why Caro is gothic for most people bc Sorel and Salvador are icons but it’s not actually what the realm about. Gothic horror is just part of the fun.
also Light did quoted “The Black Cat” from Edgar Allen Poe for the School Cat so Gothic Horror is definitely present
so you say Karen smoothie is Chaos flavored

I think the realms are represented the theme of each characters.
<:realm_chaos:1435780359852654745> : The realms that can mix with other realm like chaotic.
<:realm_caro:1435780402143826111> : Characters in this realms have a theme of blood, flesh or sacrifice, such as Uvhash based on the god with the same who has the alias as Mad Blood God of The Void, Thais who based on Shub Niggurath who gives birth endlessly etc. Agrippa who based in Eihort, the god who will force human to accept their death or carrying their children and died.
<:realm_aequor:1435780325354504242> Most characters here are involved with Cthulhu, Murphy who based on Cthylla, the daughter of Cthulhu. Goliath who based on Dagon, Miriam who based on Mother Hydra, and Caecus who based on Deep Ones. 
<:realm_ultra:1435780281385619506> Characters in this realms is involved traveling between dimensions, such as Jenkin based on Brown Jenkin, the rat who can travel between dimensions with Kesiah Mason the witch in The Dream In The Witch House. Winkle, who is Great Race Of Yith, who can transfer her the consciousness to another body through time and space.

I really like these ideas cause it doesn't use those elements such as fire water wind or earth like Deleth's idea.
oh my
you also don’t like Derleth elements
Yeah, you also don't like it?
ofc
nobody likes it
Lol
feels weird when you dump eldritch gods down into elemental system lol
also I don’t like Kthanid
and Titus Crow
True
but it’s another story
also <@551766367578095638> there are now 2 of you

i'm getting this weird feeling re-reading the now translated ch5. A lot more of the plot is clear... But the AI translation feels like it did a way better job with the penmanship. Same with desecrate. If there's anybody who can read both CN and EN and can deny/confirm this.
when did you last read ch5 and desecrate?
there have been a few revisions so far so the versions have had a bit of difference
also which stages of desecrate specifically, since currently about 1.5 stages of desecrate are human translated
I re-read desecrate up to the ||part where lemuria sinks.||
Last time i read ch5 was when it was AI translated.
It will be so inherently funny. If the parts of the sentence structure and phrasing I liked. Were dreamed up by me reading ai nonsense.
I haven't reviewed the professional translation yet so I'm not sure
at some point once the entire main story is done I'll likely see if I can do a full proper editing pass on it
there's definitely some pretty awkward bits in the couple of stages I checked to see what it was like
||like doll saying "good nappin'?"||

Yeah, cause now I can smell what you or Ace have translated or edited from a mile away.

Pollux's event story was done partly/mostly by Ace. I'd wager.
That's funnyyyyyy
https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/1402538040253087766/1422320896445648988/image.png?ex=690e580f&is=690d068f&hm=1ab002c7b4a2df8a41fc5176ed5e6d215a4e9178fc3f0f4d99556a4611a63234&
Or it might be that the CN writing also just improves over time into what I consider to be... The morimens style. With all its silly porticism.
I had to save this because it was just too silly
Poeticism*
Let me make fake words phone.

Basically this goes for both ch5 and desecrate as far as I've read. 

But the build up feels absent. It feels very... Word for word translated with no regard to what the reader is supposed to feel/what the scene leads up to.

And then emotional climaxes, the things that are very easy to emotionally read. Are done well... Because ya-know... It's a slight bit... Easier??
That's my thoughts compressed into the message to not flood the lore channel.
Thanks Lens
nah I get what you mean, it's a pretty common side effect of direct text translations without context of the presentation ingame
which is kind of what happens with professional translations since they're not playing the game, they just get the script to work on

I already knew that was an issue so for the parts I work on I always have the game open in the side to play the lines live as I translate so I can review the context, but that's not really a sustainable workflow from a professional point of view
it's something that should get fixed with a proper editing pass though
Yeah it's fine. I suppose someone like you is there to tie the knot at the end of the workflow.
I know Ace is currently editing the ch6-9 stuff so when anniversary hits they should be much better

Again... It's a big ask. But I hope the release/translation schedule can be planned around these fixes happening before they release...

Ah you've gotten ahead of me.
We love you ace. And rest of the volunteers. Go get em gang
I have some questions about the main story
so what happened on that first day at mythag? I'm still not really sure what went down, I know there was an explosion and ramona saved us
or.. i think thats what happened?

A year before the prologue. Akushnet(I'm not sure if that's the canon name now) has a dissolution problem, with a big explosion. 

In which the keeper links with Ramona and a bunch of awaker and saves the day. Before the  keeper gets brought to mythag.

Then a year later, the prologue happens. The silver key gets given, and tawil does a little silly, ¿summoning dissolution monsters and the like?
ohhh
Someone can correct me on the latter part if there's more to it.
wait that was tawil i thought it was the sculptors association
ok so in acushet we linked with a bunch of awakeners, and then a year later some stuff went down at our... tombstone key ceremoney thing
and a bunch of students perished
but what happened with Lotan? why did she go mad?
also why did tawil summon all those things
besides what the others have already stated, i also have a doc that briefly goes over the themes of each realmhttps://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1408838316153114716
all the awakeners at mythag normally link with the black pool under mythag in order to stay sane, during the prologue  it started acting up so lotan started losing it
im with robo, i thought some awakeners were able to stay sane even if unliked for longish periods of time
||like tulu||
yeah like ogier and also tulu
and doll?
and lotan is a very elder awakener too right? i think she also has a high gnosis index
i think for ogier and doll the link still works for them
oh right I know higher gnosis index is supposed to mean like, more stable? is it more stable or less stable, what does the number mean exactly
i don't think all awakeners linked to the black pool had their links cut, just some
ahhh ok that makes sense
varies by awakener but i don't think we know why or who can manage
i like that answer, we still got some mysticism c:
generally it means higher spiritual strength/closer affinity to their Higher Will, but its kinda a bullshit number that doesn't correlate directly
it probably indicates Something but as of now we're not sure what exactly
Tulu also has one of (or even) the highest recorded indices amongst all the Awakeners in game up till now
which could explain how he can ||leave Mythag and control himself without needing a link||
yeah cause i was looking at the archive definition and it says basically higher indices = more stability of spirit fragments, less madness, less loss of control, which makes sense for tulu
but what about gdoll

tbh i'm still a little confused why... but it has to do with the keeper being 'the variable' they've been waiting for (and i think it starts cause like. tawil has been asleep and then during the prologue wakes up to check out the keeper, which disturbs the black pool, but that's a guess)
oh, gdoll ||had direct contact with the higher will|| right? thats why she went mad
yeah the number has a lot of exceptions
generally its more stable mentally
but there's a lot of cases where it's not
her gnostic index is pretty high too, like 42
could mean gdoll is actually completely sane and is the most sane doll lol that would be funny
i forgot how venush explained it
arc2 ch1 gdoll is actually very sane
gdoll is high cause she submitted to pure dissolution essentially and learned forbidden knowledge
but essentially gdoll’s logic is sound, and she knows what shes doing and her reasons for doing so
ahh i see
which is why you can consider her ‘sane’
yeah her 'insanity' also isn't like regular awakener insanity
she's not losing herself like lotan
rather she's very lucid
just has some crazy ideas
psychopath vs psychosis
Air conditioner
Gdoll was just really nihilistic. Her logic is accurate from what infos she has
